title: careya australis
intro: Careya australis, also known as the white beech or Australian beech, is a species of flowering plant in the family Sapotaceae, native to Australia and New Guinea. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its bark, which has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat a variety of ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The health benefits of Careya australis include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe and protect the skin, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and improve cardiovascular health. Careya australis is often used in herbal preparations, such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
